title: Why Lewis Hamilton Needs a Specific Type of Car — Explained
description:
Why Lewis Hamilton Needs a Specific Type of Car — Explained

Lewis Hamilton has always been one of Formula 1's most unique drivers.

Throughout his career, he has repeatedly shown that he performs best when a car behaves in a very specific way.

And when that balance is missing, even one of the greatest drivers in history can struggle to extract maximum performance.

But why?

In this video, we break down:

The specific type of car Lewis Hamilton prefers

title: Formula 1 Cars, Explained with Max Verstappen
description:
This video explores the world of Formula 1 through the lens of Max Verstappen, breaking down how F1 cars are engineered for extreme speed, precision, and performance. From aerodynamics to hybrid power units, every detail is explained in a simple yet powerful way. Discover how Verstappen’s driving style maximizes cutting-edge technology, strategy, and skill to dominate the track. A thrilling deep dive into modern motorsport innovation and engineering excellence.

title: Hamilton’s Speed Surge Explained — Ferrari’s Barcelona Upgrade Exposed
description:
Lewis Hamilton's remarkable Ferrari resurgence continues to gather momentum as the Scuderia arrives in Barcelona with its biggest upgrade package of the 2026 Formula 1 season.

After securing multiple podiums and climbing to second in the championship standings, Hamilton heads into the Spanish Grand Prix with growing confidence and a Ferrari team determined to close the gap to Mercedes. With a new front wing, updated floor, and key aerodynamic developments, Barcelona could become the most import

title: The Psychology Behind Lewis Hamilton's Driving — Explained
description:
The Psychology Behind Lewis Hamilton's Driving — Explained

Lewis Hamilton is the most successful driver in Formula 1 history.

More wins. More podiums. More championship battles than almost anyone who has ever raced.

Most people assume his success came from one thing:

👉 raw speed.

But the deeper engineers studied Hamilton's career, the more they realized there was something even more important behind his dominance.

His mind.

In this video, we break down:

title: 2026 F1 Monaco GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: Although Max Verstappen's qualifying brilliance on Sunday came to nought - came to a dead stop on the grid, just after lights-out - there was still a Monaco GP to be run. There were penalties aplenty; there were Safety Cars; there was a standing re-start; and drivers like Ferrari's Charles Leclerc hit the barriers. title: 2026 F1 Canadian GP RACE Analysis
description:
2026 F1 Canadian GP RACE Analysis

Canada 2026 delivered absolute heartbreak for George Russell.

Mercedes had the pace, George looked in control, and another huge result was on the table… until the car let go. Mechanical failure ends his race in brutal fashion after what looked like a genuine shot at victory.

But there was another story unfolding before the blow-up: the front tyre battle.

George's early front-right lock-up created a much bigger headache than Antonelli��s initial front-left is

title: 2026 F1 Canadian GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: What a race!  Kimi Antonelli and George Russell fought for the lead until lap 31, when George's Mercedes ground to a halt with a battery issue. Kimi then cruised to his fourth successive victory, leaving the P2 spotlight on Sir Lewis Hamilton (Ferrari) and Max Verstappen (Red Bull-RBPT-Ford); Sir Lewis won that battle. The McLaren-Mercedes? After qualifying on row two, they were out of it virtually from the start when the team opted to start on Pirelli intermediates on a dry track. Lando Norris

title: 2026 F1 MIAMI GP race analysis by Peter Windsor
description: Astoundingly - astonishingly - Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli in Miami won his third successive Grand Prix  from his third, successive pole. title: Japanese GP ANALYSED: Pace, Starts, Drivers + Incidents COMPARED | F1 Data Analysis
description:
What did we learn from the 2026 F1 Japanese Grand Prix? Are McLaren back on form? How far off the Mercedes pace was everyone else? What caused Russell and Verstappen's issues? How bad really was the pace for Red Bull? How did Leclerc and Hamilton compare, and what about Antonelli vs Russell on pure pace? All that and more is explored in this analysis!
